Ernestia glandulosa is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and is used to treat various conditions such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ernestia glandulosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ernestia glandulosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
